response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial domain experience (founder of an open-source GIS platform used by Green Map) and proven low-latency systems work handling millions of requests per second with asynchronous I/O — but that systems work was in DLang, not C++ or Rust. His professional C++ exposure is brief and dated (a seven-month stint at National Instruments ending in 2015), his recent decade is predominantly TypeScript/Node.js product engineering, and he shows no routing, tiling, or navigation-guidance experience. Strong engineering fundamentals and demonstrated willingness to work across the stack keep him in consideration, but the core technical requirements are only partially met.
strengths: Founder-level geospatial experience building and operating an open-source GIS data platform
Low-latency distributed systems work (async I/O, caching, fibers) at millions of requests per second
Broad full-stack range with strong testing culture (TDD, CI/CD, test tooling author)
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and roughly a decade old; no Rust experience listed
No routing, map-tiling, or navigation guidance system experience
Recent roles are web/product engineering in TypeScript, not data-pipeline or systems work
